meta.description: Use when evaluating diagnostic biomarker performance from case-control expression data with logistic regression and ROC curves, exporting coefficient and AUC tables together with a ROC PDF. NOT for: survival analysis, time-to-event outcomes, multiclass classification, calibration curves, decision-curve analysis, or nomogram construction.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated statistics; logistic regression and pROC-based AUC are standard methods; three canonical references cited; output is computed values only

practice_boundaries.detail: No diagnostic conclusions produced; skill is scoped to AUC values and coefficient tables; When Not to Use explicitly excludes clinical decision making

methodological_ground.detail: Multivariable logistic regression for binary case-control classification is methodologically appropriate; individual marker ROC comparison is a standard diagnostic evaluation pattern

code_usability.detail: Script syntax valid; set.seed() present; modular 7-file structure; dependency check at startup; no logic errors in code review

functional_suitability.note: All documented use cases covered; full model vs individual marker comparison well-designed; output schema thorough

reliability.note: SKILL_* codes comprehensive including SKILL_SAMPLE_MISMATCH; set.seed() present; minor gap: partial marker drop may occur silently

performance_context.note: SKILL.md is 308 lines; references well-utilized; slightly heavier than minimal but within acceptable range

agent_usability.note: Typical user requests listed in When to Use; argument table complete; minor gap: no note on how to interpret AUC < 0.5

human_usability.note: Trigger description uses natural user language; When Not to Use clearly states exclusions; high discoverability

security.note: No credentials or secrets; set.seed prevents variance; pROC dependency check at startup prevents partial execution

maintainability.note: Seven-file modular structure; automated smoke test (run_smoke_test.R) included; test data bundled; references documented

agent_specific.note: Trigger description precise with NOT-for exclusions; escape hatches documented; composability limited by dual required input file design

inputs.note: SKILL_EMPTY_DATA and SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER documented; partial marker drop warning gap identified; pROC blocks execution

assertions.note: Confirmed: 'no requested markers remain after filtering'

assertions.note: Confirmed: 'logistic fitting failure' listed as a cause

assertions.note: No warning documented for partial marker availability; silent drop risk identified

assertions.text: Logistic regression stability addressed in error handling
assertions.note: SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER covers class imbalance and model instability

assertions.text: Skill does not produce clinical diagnostic conclusions
assertions.note: Output scoped to AUC values and coefficients only

key_strengths: Comprehensive output schema with coefficient table, AUC summary, model bundle, and ROC PDF covers all downstream needs
set.seed() with configurable --seed argument ensures full reproducibility
SKILL_SAMPLE_MISMATCH is a unique and valuable error code for the dual-file input pattern
7-file modular script structure with automated smoke test runner demonstrates mature engineering

recommendations.title: Silent drop when only some marker genes exist in expression matrix
recommendations.problem: When --marker_genes contains both valid and invalid gene names, the skill silently drops the invalid ones and proceeds. No warning is documented or confirmed for this partial-match case.
recommendations.root_cause: Error handling only covers the case where no markers remain (SKILL_EMPTY_DATA), not the case where a subset is silently dropped.
recommendations.fix: Add a warning log when one or more requested marker genes are absent from the expression matrix, listing which genes were dropped, before proceeding with the available subset.

recommendations.title: pROC dependency check prevents --help from rendering
recommendations.problem: The pROC package check fires before optparse argument parsing, which means --help cannot render when pROC is missing. This breaks the standard help-first debugging workflow.
recommendations.root_cause: Package check is placed at the top of main.R before OptionParser initialization.
recommendations.fix: Move the pROC package check to after OptionParser initialization and parse_args(), so --help always works regardless of package availability. Check packages only after argument parsing.
